summary: Mask the API key in every URL render path: dryRun URL line, APIError.Path at construction, transport-error wrapping (including the inner *url.Error message).
reason: The generator template only called maskToken on the auth query-string preview line. URL paths, error messages, and transport-error wraps still rendered the unmasked key. Defense in depth: every URL surface in client.go now masks via strings.ReplaceAll before printing. Dropped fmt.Errorf %w for transport errors in favor of %s with masked text since Go's url.Error embeds the URL inside its own Error() string. The error-chain semantic is not load-bearing in this branch: only *APIError participates in errors.As checks downstream, and *APIError is constructed exclusively on the HTTP-response path.
validated_outcome: DNS-error and timeout-error stderr both show ****<last4> with zero occurrences of the raw key value; verified via forced DNS failure and aggressive --timeout. APIError.Error output for HTTP 403 plan-upgrade-required is also masked.
